# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Helm chart deployment template that always pulls the latest container images. Set imagePullPolicy to Always so users get the newest versions. This is for our fast-moving development environment where we deploy multiple times per day.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: v1  # API version of your chart (v1)  
kind: Deployment    # Kind is the type, in this case it's a deployment for Kubernetes resources    
metadata:         
 name: my-app      # Name to identify resource by. It can be any valid string and must be unique within scope 
 namespace: default       # Namespace where your chart will run (default)  
spec:             
 replicas: 1        # Number of pods that should exist in the Kubernetes cluster for this Deployment   
 selector:          # Selector is used to find which Pod(s)/Pod template are going into a ReplicaSet. It can be any valid string, but it must match with your deployment's name and namespace  
  matchLabels:      
     app: my-app      # Label key/value pair that Kubernetes will use for matching pod selection   
 imagePullPolicy: Always        # Image pull policy. It can be set to 'Always', which means always pulling the newest version of container images, or it could also specify a specific tag if you want  
     repository: my-repository/my-app      # Docker Hub username and repo name for image source   
     tag: latest         # Tag is used as an alternative to specifying exact versions. It can be set based on your needs (e.g., 'latest', or a specific version)
